description_long: This object consists of four olive-drab, spherical hand grenades grouped together, each featuring a prominent safety lever and pull ring. The grenades have a matte, military-green finish with visible seams running around their equators and yellow stenciled text indicating their type and explosive content. The safety levers are metallic, slightly worn, and attached to the tops of the grenades, with the pull rings looped through the pins. The grenades are arranged in a cluster, with some levers overlapping and the bodies touching, suggesting they are either bundled for transport or as a makeshift multi-charge device. The overall appearance is rugged and utilitarian, with minor surface scuffs and a utilitarian, functional design. The text and markings are partially visible, providing identification and handling instructions.
description: A cluster of four olive-drab hand grenades with metal safety levers and yellow stenciled markings, arranged closely together. The grenades have a rugged, matte finish and visible pull rings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the yellow stenciled text, safety levers, and pull rings prominently on the clustered grenades.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rounded bodies and overlapping safety levers, with less visible text.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back curvature of the grenades and the arrangement of the safety levers, with no visible text.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the bottoms and sides of the grenades, with the safety levers and pull rings partially visible.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is initiated by pulling the safety pin and releasing the safety lever, which triggers an internal fuse delay.
